item.title: Fijian Drua welcome expanded 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season
item.description: The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua has welcomed today's announcement by Super Rugby Pacific confirming an expanded 2027 season, with a longer regular season and a revamped Finals Series set to bring more rugby, more excitement and higher stakes throughout the competition. The Drua says the 2027 campaign will kick off on 12th February, with each team playing 16 regular season matches across a 17-week s
item.content:encoded:
The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua has welcomed today's announcement by Super Rugby Pacific confirming an expanded 2027 season, with a longer regular season and a revamped Finals Series set to bring more rugby, more excitement and higher stakes throughout the competition.

The Drua says the 2027 campaign will kick off on 12th February, with each team playing 16 regular season matches across a 17-week season.

They say the top six teams will then advance to a new-look Finals Series, where the top two finishers will earn direct passage to the semi-finals, placing an even greater premium on strong performances throughout the regular season.

Fijian Drua CEO Jeff Miller says the expanded competition was a major win for everyone involved.

Miller says this is fantastic news for the players, their sponsors, fans and the competition as a whole.

He says more matches mean more opportunities for the Drua Nation to get behind the team, and the new Finals format rewards consistency across the entire season.

The CEO says every game will count, making the competition even more exciting.

Miller says the longer season would also create more opportunities for fans and partners to connect with the club.

He says excited about what this means for the future of Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific and can't wait to embrace the challenge as we build towards the 2027 season.

Miller says the incredible sponsors mean they will deliver even more fan activations, giveaways and prizes, while our curtain-raiser 'A Games' will showcase more of your favourite Drua players alon gside some of Fiji's top provincial rugby talent.

item.title: Ba beaten by Hekari Women FC 3-1 in OFC Women's Champions League
item.description: Discipline has cost Ba Women as they have been beaten 3-1 by Hekari Women in their Group A match of the OFC Women’s Champions League. Narieta Leba gave the Fijian side the lead in the first half at the National Stadium in Honiara, but a red card for Ba Women in second half stoppage time opened the floodgates, and Hekari flipped the contest to claim a crucial three points.
item.content:encoded:
Discipline has cost Ba Women as they have been beaten 3-1 by Hekari Women in their Group A match of the OFC Women’s Champions League.

Narieta Leba gave the Fijian side the lead in the first half at the National Stadium in Honiara, but a red card for Ba Women in second half stoppage time opened the floodgates, and Hekari flipped the contest to claim a crucial three points.

Ba was leading 1-nil at halftime but the tide shifted for Hekari in the second half.

Ba Women were reduced to 10 players after Sereana Naweni was sent off for a second yellow card in stoppage time, allowing Hekari Women to capitalise by scoring two late goals after already equalising in the 71st minute.

Maria Veronika, Nenny Elipas and Phylis Pala scored for Hekari Women.

Hekari face Henderson Eels FC in their last group game on Saturday, while Ba Women FC will take on Tafea FC, with semi-final places still up for grabs.

item.title: Mexico books Round of 16 spot with 2-0 win over Ecuador
item.description: Mexico has advanced to the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup after beating Ecuador 2-0. The match was delayed by an hour due to a thunderstorm in Mexico City before the co-hosts produced a dominant first-half display.
item.content:encoded:
Mexico has advanced to the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup after beating Ecuador 2-0.

The match was delayed by an hour due to a thunderstorm in Mexico City before the co-hosts produced a dominant first-half display.

The Mexicans made a fast start, with Julian Quinones scoring the opening goal before setting up Raul Jimenez to make it 2-0 before half-time.

Ecuador played better in the second half but could not score, with Gonzalo Plata coming closest to finding the back of the net.

With the win, Mexico will play either England or DR Congo in the Round of 16.

The match ended on a sour note for Ecuador after defender Piero Hincapie was sent off in stoppage time for covering his mouth while speaking to an opponent, becoming the second player at the tournament to be sent off for the offence after Paraguay's Miguel Almiron.

In other results, France beat Sweden 3-0 to set up a Round of 16 clash with Paraguay, while Norway advanced after edging Ivory Coast 2-1.

Meanwhile, in tomorrow’s Round of 32 matches, England will face DR Congo at 4am, Belgium will face Senegal at 8am, while the USA will take on Bosnia and Herzegovina at 12pm.

item.title: No breach of FIFA rules in Ba player's pre-match ritual - Fiji FA
item.description: The Fiji Football Association says a Roylux Realty Ba FC player did not breach the Laws of the Game or FIFA regulations after a vi

item.title: Mbappe stars as France storms into World Cup last 16
item.description: Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France cruised to a 3-0 victory over Sweden this morning to book their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Mbappe's brace took his tournament tally to six goals, drawing level with Lionel Messi in the race for the Golden Boot.
item.content:encoded:
Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France cruised to a 3-0 victory over Sweden this morning to book their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.

Mbappe's brace took his tournament tally to six goals, drawing level with Lionel Messi in the race for the Golden Boot.

France will now face Paraguay in the Round of 16 after they stunned four-time world champions Germany on penalties.

The France and Paraguay clash will kick off at 9am on Sunday.

Norway has also progressed to the Round of 16 after edging Ivory Coast 2-1 this morning.

item.title: Doge called up to replace Tagi for Flying Fijians
item.description: Swire Shipping Fijian Drua tighthead prop Mesake Doge has just been called up to the FIJI Water Flying Fijians squad for the Nations Championship. Doge has been called up to replace Luke Tagi who is unavailable due to personal commitments.
item.content:encoded:
Swire Shipping Fijian Drua tighthead prop Mesake Doge has just been called up to the FIJI Water Flying Fijians squad for the Nations Championship.

Doge has been called up to replace Luke Tagi who is unavailable due to personal commitments.

Fiji Rugby says they look forward to welcoming Tagi back into the squad in the future.

They congratulate Doge for his selection and wish him and the Flying Fijians squad all the success as they proudly represent Fiji.

The Flying Fijians take on Wales at 1.10am Sunday before facing England on July 12th at Hill Dickinson Stadium. They will then wrap up their tour against Scotland on July 19th at Murrayfield Stadium.

In other matches this weekend, New Zealand will play France at 7.10pm Saturday in Christchurch and Australia takes on Ireland at 10.10pm, while South Africa will face England at 3.40am Sunday with Argentina playing Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: Rugby more than a game as Fiji returns to Cardiff for home match on Sunday
item.description: The Nations Championship home match for the FIJI Water Flying Fijians against Wales will be more than a rugby game; it is a celebr
